title: L1: Formulas & Cell References
description: Build, understand and replicate spreadsheet formulas (Income = Quantity x Price), introduced step by step, ready for BTEC DIT Component 2 Task 2a.

steps.title: What is a Formula?
steps.content:
What is a formula?

A spreadsheet can do your maths for you. You just give it an instruction. That instruction is called a formula.

There is one golden rule:

Every formula starts with an equals sign =

When you press Enter, the cell shows the answer – not the formula. You can still see the formula in the formula bar at the top of the sheet.

Worked example

Type =5+3 into a cell and press Enter:

What you typeWhat the cell showsWhat the formula bar shows
=5+38=5+3

Now you try

In the sheet below, click cell B2, type =5+3 and press Enter. Watch the formula bar at the top. Then click Check.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=14',function(){var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l1-try-sheet'),{data:[['','',''],['','',''],['','','']],columns:[{width:110},{width:110},{width:110}],minDimensions:[3,3],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l1-try-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l1-try-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l1-try-fb');var found=false;for(var y=0;y<3;y++){for(var x=0;x<3;x++){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(x,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(x,y,true));if(raw.charAt(0)==='='&&Math.abs(val-8)<0.001){found=true;}}}if(found){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Perfect. =5+3 gives 8. The cell shows the answer; the formula bar shows the formula.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Type =5+3 into a cell (do not forget the = sign) and press Enter, then Check.';}};});});});})();

steps.title: The Four Operators
steps.content:
The four maths operators

A formula can add, subtract, multiply or divide. The symbols are a little different from the ones you write by hand:

To do thisUse this symbolExampleAnswer
Add+=4+610
Subtract-=10-28
Multiply* (not ×)=5*315
Divide/ (not ÷)=20/45

The two to remember: multiply is the star * and divide is the slash /.

Your turn

Write each formula. Start with = and pick the right symbol. Spaces do not matter.
steps.js:
(function(){var qs=[{q:'Write a formula that adds 4 and 6.',p:'^=\\s*(4\\s*\\+\\s*6|6\\s*\\+\\s*4)\\s*$',hint:'=4+6'},{q:'Write a formula that multiplies 5 by 3.',p:'^=\\s*(5\\s*\\*\\s*3|3\\s*\\*\\s*5)\\s*$',hint:'=5*3'},{q:'Write a formula that subtracts 2 from 10.',p:'^=\\s*10\\s*-\\s*2\\s*$',hint:'=10-2'},{q:'Write a formula that divides 20 by 4.',p:'^=\\s*20\\s*/\\s*4\\s*$',hint:'=20/4'}];var c=document.getElementById('l1-ops');c.innerHTML=qs.map(function(x,i){return '';}).join('')+'
';document.getElementById('l1-ops-c').onclick=function(){var ok=0;qs.forEach(function(x,i){var el=document.getElementById('l1-ops-'+i);var pass=new RegExp(x.p).test(el.value.trim());el.classList.toggle('correct',pass);el.classList.toggle('wrong',!pass);if(pass)ok++;});var fb=document.getElementById('l1-ops-fb');if(ok===qs.length){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='All correct. You can now build any calculation.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='You have '+ok+' / '+qs.length+'. Remember: * for multiply, / for divide, and always start with =.';}};})();

steps.title: Pointing at Cells
steps.content:
Pointing at cells instead of numbers

So far we put numbers straight into the formula, like =5*3. But spreadsheets become really powerful when we point at the cells that hold the numbers instead.

=A2*B2 means: “multiply whatever is in cell A2 by whatever is in cell B2”.

Worked example

ABC
1PriceQuantityTotal
223=A2*B2 → 6

Here is the magic: if you change the Price or the Quantity, the Total updates by itself. You never have to work it out again. This is exactly what BTEC Component 2 needs.

See it happen

Cell C2 below already contains =A2*B2 and shows 6. Change the Quantity in B2 to 10 and press Enter. Watch the Total change on its own, then click Check.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=14',function(){var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l1-ref-sheet'),{data:[['Price (£)','Quantity','Total (£)'],[2,3,'=A2*B2']],columns:[{width:100},{width:100},{width:110}],minDimensions:[3,2],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l1-ref-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l1-ref-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l1-ref-fb');var total=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(2,1,true));if(Math.abs(total-20)<0.001){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Exactly! You did not retype the answer - the formula recalculated by itself. That is why we point at cells.';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Change B2 to 10 so the Total becomes 20, then click Check. The formula =A2*B2 does the rest.';}};});});});})();

steps.title: Income (Worked Example)
steps.content:
Putting it together: Income from sales

In BTEC Component 2 you are given a company’s sales data and asked to work out the Income from sales for each item. Income is just:

Income = Quantity × Price

Click through how to build the formula for the first item.
steps.js:
(function(){var steps=[{t:'1. The data',h:'
ABCD
1ItemQuantityPriceIncome
2Pens120.50?
',n:'We want the Income for Pens. It goes in cell D2.'},{t:'2. The thinking',h:'

Income = Quantity × Price

',n:'The Quantity is in B2. The Price is in C2.'},{t:'3. Build the formula',h:'

=B2*C2

',n:'Start with =, point at B2, use * to multiply, then point at C2.'},{t:'4. The result',h:'
Pens120.506.00
',n:'12 × 0.50 = 6.00. The cell shows 6, the formula bar shows =B2*C2. Next step: your turn.'}];var i=0,c=document.getElementById('l1-step');function render(){var s=steps[i];c.innerHTML='

'+s.t+'

'+s.h+'

'+s.n+'

Step '+(i+1)+' of '+steps.length+'
';document.getElementById('l1-prev').onclick=function(){if(i>0){i--;render();}};document.getElementById('l1-next').onclick=function(){if(i

steps.title: Income (Your Turn)
steps.content:
Your turn: calculate the Income

You just saw how to write =B2*C2 for Pens. Now do the same for every item in the Income column (column D).

Click D2, type =B2*C2, press Enter. Then D3 is =B3*C3, and so on for each row.

Tip: the formula bar at the top always shows the formula in the cell you have selected.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=14',function(){var data=[['Item','Quantity','Price (£)','Income (£)'],['Pens',12,0.5,''],['Notebooks',8,1.2,''],['Erasers',20,0.3,''],['Folders',5,1.5,'']];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l1-income-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:120},{width:90},{width:90},{width:110}],minDimensions:[4,5],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l1-income-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l1-income-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l1-income-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l1-income-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l1-income-fb');var rows=[[1,12,0.5],[2,8,1.2],[3,20,0.3],[4,5,1.5]];var usedFormula=true,allOk=true;for(var i=0;i<rows.length;i++){var y=rows[i][0];var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,true));var expect=rows[i][1]*rows[i][2];if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')usedFormula=false;if(isNaN(val)||Math.abs(val-expect)>0.005)allOk=false;}if(!usedFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Use a FORMULA in every Income cell (start with =). Pens is =B2*C2, Notebooks is =B3*C3, and so on.';return;}if(!allOk){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Some Income values are not right yet. Income = Quantity × Price for that row.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Brilliant - every Income is worked out with a formula. That is the exact skill marked in Task 2a.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Replicating a Formula
steps.content:
Replicating (copying) a formula

Typing =B2*C2, then =B3*C3, then =B4*C4 by hand is slow. BTEC asks you to write the formula once and then replicate it. Replicate just means copy it down.

The clever bit

When you copy a formula down one row, the cell references move with it automatically:

CellFormula after copying down
D2=B2*C2 (the one you typed)
D3=B3*C3 (copied)
D4=B4*C4 (copied)

How to replicate

1. Click the cell that already has the formula (D2).
2. Copy it: Ctrl + C.
3. Select the empty cells below (D3 to D5).
4. Paste: Ctrl + V.

Your turn

D2 below already contains =B2*C2. Replicate it down so every row has an Income, then click Check.

Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=14',function(){var data=[['Item','Quantity','Price (£)','Income (£)'],['Rulers',15,0.4,'=B2*C2'],['Markers',6,1.1,''],['Glue sticks',10,0.8,''],['Scissors',4,2.5,'']];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l1-rep-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:120},{width:90},{width:90},{width:110}],minDimensions:[4,5],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l1-rep-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l1-rep-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l1-rep-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l1-rep-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l1-rep-fb');var rows=[[2,6,1.1],[3,10,0.8],[4,4,2.5]];var usedFormula=true,allOk=true;for(var i=0;i<rows.length;i++){var y=rows[i][0];var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(3,y,true));var expect=rows[i][1]*rows[i][2];if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')usedFormula=false;if(isNaN(val)||Math.abs(val-expect)>0.005)allOk=false;}if(!usedFormula){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Copy the formula in D2 and paste it into D3, D4 and D5 - do not type the answers.';return;}if(!allOk){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - make sure you replicated D2 into every row below it.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Replicated perfectly. Each row used its own =B?*C? - the references adjusted by themselves.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

These questions cover everything from this lesson. Feedback appears after each one.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l1-exam',[{q:'Every spreadsheet formula must start with which symbol?',options:['=','+','#','@'],answer:0,right:'Correct. All formulas start with =.'},{q:'Which symbol means multiply?',options:['*','x','%','&'],answer:0,right:'Correct. * means multiply.'},{q:'What does =A2*B2 mean?',options:['Multiply the value in A2 by the value in B2','Add A2 and B2','Print the letters A2 B2','Multiply 2 by 2'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Income = Quantity × Price. Quantity is in B2 and Price is in C2. Which formula is correct?',options:['=B2*C2','=B2+C2','=Quantity*Price','B2*C2'],answer:0,right:'Correct - and never forget the = sign.'},{q:'You copy =B2*C2 from D2 down into D3. What does it become?',options:['=B3*C3','=B2*C2','=C2*D2','=B4*C4'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The references move down with the formula.'},{q:'Why is =A2*B2 better than typing =2*3 directly?',options:['If the numbers change, the answer updates automatically','It looks nicer','It is shorter to type','It uses less memory'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Cell references keep results up to date.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row correctly to finish. If you get one wrong, the streak resets - so take your time.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'Quantity is in B2, Price is in C2. Formula for Income?',o:['=B2*C2','=B2+C2','=C2-B2','B2*C2'],a:0},{q:'Which symbol divides?',o:['/','*','-','+'],a:0},{q:'A formula always starts with...',o:['=','+','!','?'],a:0},{q:'Copy =D2*E2 down one row. It becomes...',o:['=D3*E3','=D2*E2','=E2*F2','=D1*E1'],a:0},{q:'Cost is in A4, VAT is in B4. Formula to add them?',o:['=A4+B4','=A4*B4','=A4-B4','A4+B4'],a:0},{q:'Which symbol means multiply?',o:['*','x','/','+'],a:0},{q:'Total in C2 = Price A2 times Quantity B2. Formula?',o:['=A2*B2','=A2/B2','=A2+B2','=A2-B2'],a:0},{q:'Why use cell references in formulas?',o:['Results update when the data changes','They look colourful','They are faster to delete','They hide the data'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l1-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete. Well done!':'Correct. Next question loading.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - your streak resets. Try again.';setTimeout(render,1100);}};});}render();})();

quizQuestions.q: A spreadsheet formula must start with which symbol?
Which symbol should you use to multiply in a spreadsheet formula?
In the spreadsheet, use a formula in D2 to calculate Income for Pens. Income = Quantity * Price.
Complete the Income column using formulas. Each row should multiply Quantity by Price for that row.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: D2 =B2*C2, then replicate down to D5.

quizQuestions.q: If you copy =B2*C2 from D2 down into D3, what should it become?
D2 has already been done. Replicate the formula down so D3 and D4 calculate the correct Income.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: Copy D2 and paste into D3:D4, or enter =B3*C3 and =B4*C4.

quizQuestions.q: Why is =B2*C2 better than typing =12*0.5 for the Pens income?
options: If Quantity or Price changes, the Income updates automatically

quizQuestions.q: Use a formula in D2 to add the Price and Delivery Cost together. Total Cost = Price + Delivery Cost.
